items.headline: Play-In Tournament Tips Off Tonight!
items.summary: The highly anticipated NBA Play-In Tournament begins today, April 14, 2026, with two crucial matchups. The Miami Heat will battle the Charlotte Hornets in the Eastern Conference, while the Portland Trail Blazers will face the Phoenix Suns in the West. These games will determine who moves one step closer to securing a playoff berth.

items.headline: LeBron, Giannis, Kawhi: Superstar Shake-Up Looms?
items.summary: NBA front offices are buzzing with speculation that superstars LeBron James, Giannis Antetokounmpo, and Kawhi Leonard could all be on the move next season. Reports indicate growing anticipation that one, two, or even all three could land with new teams, potentially reshaping the league's power landscape.

items.headline: Avdija Leads Blazers to Play-In Spot
items.summary: Deni Avdija delivered a stellar performance with 25 points, 10 assists, and 6 rebounds, guiding the POR Trail Blazers to a 122-110 victory over SAC and securing the 8th seed in the Western Conference Play-In Tournament.

items.headline: Jokic Reaches 65 Games, Secures Eligibility
items.summary: Nikola Jokic played his 65th game of the season, scoring 23 points in the first half along with 8 rebounds and 2 assists, making him eligible for NBA awards as DEN defeated SAS 128-118 and clinched the No. 3 seed.

items.headline: Scheierman's 30-Point Effort
items.summary: Baylor Scheierman led BOS with a team-high 30 points, 7 rebounds, and 7 assists in their 113-108 win over ORL.

items.headline: Garza's Dominant Double-Double
items.summary: Luka Garza tallied 27 points, 12 rebounds, and knocked down 3 three-pointers for ORL against BOS.

items.headline: Knueppel's Record-Breaking Rookie Season
items.summary: Kon Knueppel of the CHA Hornets had the most efficient rookie season in NBA history and finished third in the league in made three-pointers (3.4 per game).

items.headline: Jokic Claims Rebounding and Assist Titles
items.summary: Nikola Jokic capped off another triple-double season, leading the NBA in both rebounding (12.9 RPG) and assists (10.7 APG).

items.headline: Tonight's Play-In Tournament Schedule
items.summary: The Play-In Tournament commences with the MIA Heat vs. CHA Hornets at 7:30 PM ET, followed by the POR Trail Blazers vs. PHX Suns at 10:00 PM ET. Both games will be broadcast on Amazon Prime Video.

items.headline: Embiid Out for Play-In with Appendectomy
items.summary: The Philadelphia 76ers will be without star center Joel Embiid, who is sidelined with an appendectomy and will miss their crucial Play-In Tournament game against the Orlando Magic.

items.headline: Lillard and Grant Sidelined for Blazers Play-In
items.summary: The Portland Trail Blazers will be without Damian Lillard (left Achilles tendon, injury management) and potentially Jerami Grant (questionable, right calf strain) for their Play-In game against the Suns.

items.headline: Heat Face Play-In with Key Injuries
items.summary: The Miami Heat will be without Nikola Jovic (left ankle sprain) and Dru Smith (right foot soreness) for their Play-In matchup against the Hornets.

items.headline: First Round Playoff Matchups Taking Shape
items.summary: Detroit and Boston hold the top seeds. The DEN Nuggets will face the MIN Timberwolves in the West's 3-vs-6 matchup, while the LAL Lakers (4th seed) will take on the HOU Rockets. In the East, the NYK Knicks (3rd) play the ATL Hawks (6th), and CLE Cavaliers (4th) square off against TOR Raptors (5th).
